title: The One Where Rachel Tells...

jokes.text: No, I already packed. The only thing I couldn't find, though, was your Speedo.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions she packed for Chandler but couldn't find one item
jokes.punchline: The item is a Speedo, which Chandler clearly doesn't own/wear
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor playing on Chandler's conservative/awkward nature versus sexy swimwear

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I packed for you, but I couldn't find your Speedo.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's because I don't own a Speedo.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, that's what I figured.

jokes.text: Speedo? I don't have a Speedo. I'm gonna go pack my regular, long bathing suit.
jokes.setup: Monica's suggestion that he owns a Speedo
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward clarification about his 'regular, long bathing suit'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - awkward, overly specific, and endearingly unsexy

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't own a Speedo. I have a regular, long bathing suit.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's just what you call it.

jokes.text: And I'm gonna be an uncle! Come here. -You're all gonna be aunts and uncles. -Yeah, but I'm the only one related by blood.
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrating being aunts/uncles to Rachel's baby
jokes.punchline: Rachel matter-of-factly pointing out Joey's the only blood relative
jokes.explanation: Mild deflation of the group's excitement with logical reality check

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, you guys... Joey's the only blood relative to the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? No I'm not!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes you are. You're my half-brother.

jokes.text: Why am I talking like this?
jokes.setup: Phoebe reacting dramatically to news
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about her own theatrical speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-moment where Phoebe catches herself being overly dramatic - very character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's terrible! I mean, why would they do that? It's like the universe is conspiring against us, you know? It's all connected, like the chakras, and the energy, and... wait, am I doing that thing again where I talk like I'm in a soap opera?

jokes.text: What?! I am an excellent secret-keeper. I have kept all our secrets.
jokes.setup: Monica says Phoebe can't keep secrets
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's indignant defense immediately followed by...
jokes.explanation: Sets up the contradiction that pays off in the next beat

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can keep secrets! I'm very good at keeping secrets.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, you told everyone about my new apartment before I could tell them myself.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That was different! I thought it was public information.

jokes.text: -What secrets? -No, no, Joey. I'm not gonna tell you, because I am an excellent secret-keeper.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming to be great at keeping secrets
jokes.punchline: Immediately almost revealing secrets while insisting she doesn't reveal secrets
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - Phoebe proving Monica's point while denying it

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm great at keeping secrets. I haven't told anyone that Monica is—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? I stopped myself. And I would never tell anybody that Ross—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, I know. See? I'm keeping it in.

jokes.text: -You'll tell me later? -You already know.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if she'll tell him the secrets later
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logic that he already knows them so she's not revealing anything
jokes.explanation: Clever Phoebe logic that technically makes sense but misses the point

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Will you tell me the secrets later?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, you already know them, so I'm not really revealing anything.

jokes.text: -Maybe you and I should do something. -All in good time, my love. All in good time.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting they should follow their friends' relationship milestones
jokes.punchline: Mike's overly formal, patronizing response
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Mike's dismissive formality

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We should follow our friends' relationship milestones.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Well, that's a very interesting perspective, Phoebe. However, I believe we should establish our own timeline based on mutual understanding and personal readiness, rather than succumbing to social pressure or external benchmarks.

jokes.text: -Maybe they don't trust us. They let me keep my key the last time they were out of town. The time you broke the ketchup bottle and cleaned it with Monica's guest towels?
jokes.setup: Wondering why they don't have keys anymore
jokes.punchline: Phoebe revealing Mike's previous violation of Monica's cleanliness standards
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the real reason for the missing keys with Monica-specific details

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, Mike, remember when you ate a sandwich over Monica's white couch and got mayo on it?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: That was one time!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Exactly. One time was enough for Monica to revoke your key privileges.

jokes.text: -Hey, I washed those. -No, you didn't. Yeah, that didn't sound like me.
jokes.setup: Mike defending his cleaning efforts
jokes.punchline: Immediate self-aware admission that washing towels isn't his character
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor with good timing and character awareness

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I do wash the towels!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, you don't.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: You're right, I don't.

jokes.text: -What stuff? -Monica's chicken parm. I'll take care of it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking what stuff Mike has in the apartment
jokes.punchline: His priority is leftover food, not anything important
jokes.explanation: Mild food-focused humor typical for Friends characters

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So what stuff do you have in the apartment?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Well, I've got my guitar, some books, my collection of vinyl records...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What about the most important thing?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: My leftover lasagna in the fridge.

jokes.text: Gas leak. Oh, and bring garlic bread.
jokes.setup: Joey calling the super about a fake emergency
jokes.punchline: Casually adding a food request to the fake emergency call
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - lies for food with zero shame or subtlety

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, it's Joey Tribbiani in 4G. Yeah, I got an emergency here. My toilet's overflowing and there's water everywhere!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, it's bad. Oh, and hey, while you're coming up, could you grab me a sandwich? A turkey sandwich? Actually, make it roast beef.

jokes.text: Damn. They left without my pass to the Nassau Fossil and Natural History Museum. There goes the honeymoon.
jokes.setup: Ross realizing he forgot something for the honeymoon
jokes.punchline: It's a nerdy museum pass, which he considers essential to a romantic trip
jokes.explanation: Pure Ross character comedy - prioritizing paleontology over romance

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh no, I forgot my museum pass! We can't go on the honeymoon without it!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, it's our honeymoon.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, and it's going to be so romantic. The Natural History Museum has a new wing on Paleolithic tools. We can't miss that!

jokes.text: Wow, I thought you'd be a little more shocked.
jokes.setup: Joey expecting dramatic reaction to his revelation
jokes.punchline: Ross's complete lack of surprise about Joey sleeping with Rachel
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Joey's wounded ego at not shocking anyone

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I slept with Rachel!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, I know.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know?!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, everyone knows.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. And?!
jokes.setup: Joey pointing out Ross isn't shocked enough
jokes.punchline: Ross's exaggerated fake shock delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy with good timing

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You gotta be more shocked, man. Like this.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: Man, you know what I have to realize? Maybe I'm not the type of guy women can have just one night with. You know, they always seem to want a little bit more.
jokes.setup: Joey analyzing his relationship patterns
jokes.punchline: His narcissistic conclusion that he's too irresistible for casual relationships
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey self-aggrandizement completely missing the real situation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what my problem is? I'm too good-looking. Women see me and they immediately think 'relationship,' not 'casual dating.' I mean, look at me. How could anyone just keep this as a fling?

jokes.text: -They're kissing. Let's just go around them. -Leave them alone. They're in love. I'm in love too. But in an orderly fashion.
jokes.setup: Couple kissing blocking airport line
jokes.punchline: Chandler's distinction between chaotic love and 'orderly' love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line about controlling emotions and maintaining social order

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could they BE any more in the way?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Aww, that's sweet. They're in love.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, their love is blocking the line. I like my love orderly, with good lighting and comfortable seating.

jokes.text: They bumped them up to first class because they're on their honeymoon!
jokes.setup: Monica observing another couple getting upgraded
jokes.punchline: Sets up the competitive dynamic that will drive the scene
jokes.explanation: Setup more than punchline, establishes Monica's competitive nature

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did you see that? They just got upgraded to first class! Why didn't we get upgraded?

jokes.text: -Let's act like we're on our honeymoon. -We are on our honeymoon.
jokes.setup: Monica's scheme to get upgraded
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal-minded response pointing out they actually are
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response highlighting Monica's scheming mentality

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We should go to the hotel and complain about something so we can get upgraded.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But we're already married.

jokes.text: Grab my ass.
jokes.setup: Monica's instruction to sell their honeymoon performance
jokes.punchline: Her blunt, unromantic direction for fake affection
jokes.explanation: Monica's transactional approach to romance delivered with military efficiency

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, when he comes in, I want you to run up and kiss me like you mean it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Not like you actually like me or anything, just... convincingly.

jokes.text: See? If we'd gone around them like I said, she'd have given us those tickets! Damn it!
jokes.setup: Their plan backfiring spectacularly
jokes.punchline: Chandler's vindication and frustration at being right about going around
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup with Chandler's 'I told you so' energy

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I told you we should go around! But no, you all had to go through the middle like some kind of herd of confused cattle.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chandler, we didn't know there was going to be a—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A what? A disaster? A catastrophe? Because I predicted that! I literally said, 'This will end badly,' and you all looked at me like I was insane!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We get it, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Do you? Do you really? Because I'm not feeling the 'getting it.' I'm feeling a lot of not listening to Chandler, which is apparently the default setting for this group.

jokes.text: 25J and K, any chance those aren't together?
jokes.setup: They've been assigned regular seats after the scheme failed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's resigned question about whether they'll even sit together
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflated honeymoon energy with Chandler's pessimistic expectation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So... are we even going to sit together?

jokes.text: Of course I do! And I'm gonna get it back to you.. just as soon as they're done with it at the key-shining place.
jokes.setup: Mike trusting Phoebe with his key
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's ridiculous excuse about a 'key-shining place'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Phoebe logic creating a fake business to cover her lie

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: You can trust me with your key.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I know I can. But I have to take it to this place... it's a key-shining place. They make your keys all shiny and new.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: A key-shining place?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's in Brooklyn. You wouldn't know it.

jokes.text: -You said there was a gas leak. -Why not use your key? By the time I found it on this thing, the whole place might have exploded.
jokes.setup: Super questioning Joey's method for entering apartment
jokes.punchline: Joey's logic that finding keys takes too long during gas emergency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake emergency with Joey's commitment to the bit

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Treeger
dialogue.line: Joey, why did you break down my door?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: There was a gas emergency! I didn't have time to find my keys.

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Treeger
dialogue.line: So you just smashed through the door?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was panicking! You know how long it takes to find keys?

jokes.text: If that happens at another building I manage, people will ask questions.
jokes.setup: Discussing potential apartment explosion
jokes.punchline: Treeger's casual admission this has happened before
jokes.explanation: Dark humor suggesting Treeger has a history of building explosions delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Treeger
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, it's not the first time this has happened.

jokes.text: Well, we could have done that.
jokes.setup: Watching Treeger break down the door with power tools
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's understated observation about their overcomplicated approach
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation humor pointing out their unnecessary elaborate scheme

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, you could've just used a key.

jokes.text: -Oh, my God! Oranges! -Nice to see you again.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler trying to sneak into first-class lounge
jokes.punchline: Monica's overenthusiastic reaction to basic fruit betraying their non-first-class status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica behavior - unable to play it cool when confronted with luxury

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, look at all this fruit!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, don't—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: There's a pineapple! An actual pineapple!

dialogue.speaker: Attendant
dialogue.line: Can I help you find something?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're not supposed to be here, are we?

jokes.text: Yes, of course. Would you move your thumb? I can't see the seat number. That's all right. I have it memorized. It's 1A.
jokes.setup: Attendant asking to see their tickets
jokes.punchline: Chandler's increasingly desperate lies culminating in obviously fake first-class seat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of panic leading to obviously false claim

dialogue.speaker: Attendant
dialogue.line: Can I see your tickets, please?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, the tickets? Yeah, they're... they're in my other pants.

dialogue.speaker: Attendant
dialogue.line: Sir, you're wearing pants.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Right, well these are my OTHER other pants. I have a lot of pants.

dialogue.speaker: Attendant
dialogue.line: I'm going to need to see some ID.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: ID? Sure! I'm... I'm actually the pilot. Captain... Chandler Bing. And this is my first-class seat right here. See? It has a massage chair. And caviar. And a... a hot tub.

jokes.text: -Sir, this isn't a first-class ticket. I'm sorry. -Apology accepted. Excuse us.
jokes.setup: Getting caught with fake tickets
jokes.punchline: Chandler's attempt to graciously accept the apology as if it resolves everything
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler response trying to maintain dignity while clearly caught

dialogue.speaker: Attendant
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry, sir. Those tickets are counterfeit. You can't go in.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, well, thank you for the apology. That really means a lot. I feel so much better now that you've said sorry. In fact, I'm going to frame that apology and hang it in my apartment right next to my fake tickets.

jokes.text: Ross and Rachel. Rachel and Ross. That's been one heck of a seesaw, hasn't it?
jokes.setup: Ross trying to address their relationship history
jokes.punchline: His awkward 'seesaw' metaphor for their tumultuous relationship
jokes.explanation: Awkward Ross attempt at being profound about their relationship with childish metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's like a seesaw, you know? Up and down, up and down...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Sometimes you're on top, sometimes I'm on top...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And sometimes we both just jump off and there's this moment where nobody's on the seesaw and it's just... there.

jokes.text: Seriously, what?
jokes.setup: Ross's completely wrong assumption about why she wants to talk
jokes.punchline: Rachel's genuine confusion at his romantic misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing the complete disconnect between characters

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, we need to talk.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes! Yes, I know what this is about. Rachel, I'm so glad you brought this up because I have been wanting to tell you how I feel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You do?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes! I'm in love with you. I have been for so long and I just couldn't hold it in anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross... what are you talking about?

jokes.text: I'm pregnant.
jokes.setup: Ross's completely wrong romantic assumption
jokes.punchline: The simple statement that completely derails everything he just said
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and structure - two words that completely obliterate Ross's assumptions

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know you're still in love with me.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm married.

jokes.text: Ross?
jokes.setup: Rachel's pregnancy announcement
jokes.punchline: The implied silent reaction from Ross who has clearly frozen/fainted
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat inferred from Rachel's confused follow-up

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm pregnant.

jokes.text: What?! Well, they should put that on the box!
jokes.setup: Learning condoms are only 97% effective
jokes.punchline: Ross's indignant consumer complaint about inadequate labeling
jokes.explanation: Good Ross response deflecting emotional situation into consumer advocacy

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Only 97% effective? That's not good enough! I mean, what if I bought a parachute that was only 97% effective? I'd be very upset!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So why is it okay for condoms?

jokes.text: -They do. -No, they don't!
jokes.setup: Ross claiming condom boxes don't warn about failure rate
jokes.punchline: Rachel matter-of-factly correcting him, Ross's continued denial
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Ross's denial and misdirected anger

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: They don't put a warning on the box!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes, they do. It says right on there: 'Ninety-seven percent effective.'

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not a warning!

jokes.text: Well, they should put it in huge block letters!
jokes.setup: Accepting that it is on the box but being unsatisfied
jokes.punchline: Ross's continued consumer outrage demanding bigger warnings
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's misdirected anger at packaging instead of situation

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It says 'Some Assembly Required' on the box, but I've been working on this for three hours!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: They need bigger warnings. Like, 'WARNING: This will destroy your will to live' or 'CAUTION: You will question every decision that led you here.'

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And there should be a picture of a man crying. Just a grown man, tears streaming down his face, holding a mysterious Allen wrench.

jokes.text: -Ross, let's just forget about the condoms. -Well, I may as well have!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to move past the condom discussion
jokes.punchline: Ross's bitter pun about having 'forgotten' to use them since they don't work
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with Ross turning Rachel's phrase into bitter self-recrimination

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Can we please just move past the condom discussion?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh sure, yeah. I've actually forgotten all about them since they don't work anyway.

jokes.text: I'm indignant as a consumer!
jokes.setup: Rachel saying he seems freaked out
jokes.punchline: Ross's reframing of panic as consumer rights violation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross line - intellectualizing emotional panic into consumer advocacy speak

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You seem freaked out.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not freaked out. I'm just... exercising my right as a consumer to express my dissatisfaction with a product or service.

jokes.text: In fact, I wanna talk.. to the president of the condom company.
jokes.setup: Ross's consumer indignation building
jokes.punchline: His escalation to wanting executive-level complaint resolution
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's misdirected corporate anger

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I want to speak to a manager.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? I want to speak to the manager's manager.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: In fact, I want to speak to whoever runs this entire operation. I want the CEO. I want the president of the company on the phone right now!

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll press one!
jokes.setup: Ross apparently calling the condom company
jokes.punchline: His aggressive interaction with automated phone system
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with Ross actually following through on corporate complaint

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, hello, I'd like to speak to someone about a defective condom... No, I don't want to press 1 for English, I'm already speaking English!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Look, I don't have time for this menu system. This is urgent!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, I will NOT hold. Do you have any idea what's at stake here?!

jokes.text: Because, you know, they don't have any kids of their own.. and this door was like a child to them.
jokes.setup: Asking Treeger not to tell Monica and Chandler about the broken door
jokes.punchline: Joey's ridiculous anthropomorphization of the door as their child
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Joey logic creating fake emotional stakes for inanimate object

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Mr. Treeger, please don't tell Monica and Chandler about the door.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Treeger
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Because they're gonna kill me! Look, what if we just don't say anything? It'll be like it never happened.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Treeger
dialogue.line: The door's completely destroyed, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, I know. But think about it from the door's perspective. It's confused, it's scared... it's in a lot of pain.

jokes.text: It's looking like an all-day job. I'm gonna have to cancel my yoga class.
jokes.setup: Treeger discussing the door repair work
jokes.punchline: Revealing the tough building super does yoga
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - tough guy doing gentle activity

dialogue.speaker: Treeger
dialogue.line: Yeah, I do yoga. You got a problem with that?

dialogue.speaker: Treeger
dialogue.line: It's for flexibility and inner peace, alright?

jokes.text: -Oh, my God! What happened to the door? -So it's noticeable, huh?
jokes.setup: Ross seeing the completely destroyed door
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual question about whether massive destruction is 'noticeable'
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement for what must be obvious destruction

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God! The door!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? What's wrong with the door?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What's wrong with it?! It's completely destroyed!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So you think the landlord's gonna notice?

jokes.text: -Holy mother of God! -Oh, my God, I can't believe that! -That is brand-new information!
jokes.setup: Ross revealing Rachel is pregnant
jokes.punchline: Joey's formal, newscaster-like reaction to information he already knew
jokes.explanation: Joey's over-formal reaction while pretending not to know creates good irony

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel's pregnant.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Rachel's pregnant?!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, you knew that.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I didn't know that *you* knew that I knew that.

jokes.text: -You already know, don't you? -Little bit.
jokes.setup: Ross realizing Joey's fake surprise
jokes.punchline: Joey's minimal admission of prior knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good deflation with Joey's sheepish understatement

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You knew about this the whole time, didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I may have heard some things.

jokes.text: But the complaint department at the condom company got an earful.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he handled the pregnancy news badly
jokes.punchline: Ross's admission he called to complain to condom company instead of supporting Rachel
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Ross actually followed through on his misdirected anger

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I called the condom company to complain about a defective product instead of being there for Rachel.

jokes.text: Hey. Hold on! Are you serious? So, like, 3 percent of the time, they don't even work? They should put that on the box!
jokes.setup: Joey learning about condom failure rates
jokes.punchline: Joey having the exact same reaction as Ross about box labeling
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing both men have same consumer outrage priorities

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, so condoms don't work like 100% of the time?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, they're about 97% effective.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, so like the same odds as the box saying 'one size fits all'?

jokes.text: -Evidently, they do. -What?!
jokes.setup: Joey's complaint about box labeling
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's correction and Joey's shock, mirroring Ross's earlier reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback completing the pattern with Joey having identical reaction

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why does it say 'Fragile' on the box? There's nothing fragile in there!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's because it says 'Fragile,' not 'Handle with care.' Fragile means it breaks easily.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, what? I've been throwing these boxes around for twenty minutes!

jokes.text: Well, I'll be.
jokes.setup: Learning he was wrong about box labeling
jokes.punchline: Joey's mild, folksy acceptance after his outrage
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation with Joey's simple acceptance after his dramatic reaction

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was wrong. I accept that. Moving on.

jokes.text: No! You have been screwing us all day!
jokes.setup: Hotel offering them honeymoon suite after all the airport problems
jokes.punchline: Monica's angry rejection of good news due to accumulated frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness and inability to accept good fortune after feeling wronged

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No! No, we don't want the honeymoon suite! We don't want anything from you! We just want to go to our room, pack our bags, and get out of here!

jokes.text: -Who are you? -We're you. Just 10 seconds later!
jokes.setup: Monica confronting the other honeymoon couple
jokes.punchline: Other couple's logical response about timing
jokes.explanation: Good logical comeback highlighting the arbitrary nature of timing-based privilege

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Excuse me, but we booked this honeymoon suite first!

dialogue.speaker: Other woman
dialogue.line: Actually, we got married yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Other man
dialogue.line: So technically, we're on our honeymoon right now.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, so are we!

dialogue.speaker: Other woman
dialogue.line: Yeah, but ours just started.

jokes.text: You're not the only ones on your honeymoon!
jokes.setup: Monica's growing frustration with preferential treatment
jokes.punchline: Her desperate assertion of equal romantic status
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature making her lose sight of what should be romantic

jokes.text: We don't care about where we stay. We're here to celebrate our love for each other. We don't have to get free stuff. We just want to be together.
jokes.setup: Contrast with Monica and Chandler's materialistic approach
jokes.punchline: The other couple's genuinely romantic perspective making Monica/Chandler look petty
jokes.explanation: Good contrast highlighting Monica's misplaced priorities versus genuine romance

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We need to think about our future. A house, investments, security.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, we can't just wing it like some people.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what Mike and I do? We just look into each other's eyes and feel grateful for the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Life's about connection, not things.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Right, but what about when you get older and—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: As long as we have each other, that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's... that's really nice, actually.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So we're the materialistic ones now?

jokes.text: We need the stuff.
jokes.setup: Other couple's romantic speech about not needing material things
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt, honest admission of materialism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica line - brutally honest about prioritizing material benefits over romance

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's beautiful. I want that. I want someone to say that to me. But I also want a really nice apartment in Manhattan, and clothes, and I want to be able to go out to dinner whenever I want. So, you know, ideally I'd like both.

jokes.text: -What? Are you serious? Joey smells gas. -What else is new?
jokes.setup: Joey calling about gas emergency
jokes.punchline: Chandler's casual dismissal suggesting Joey frequently smells things
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Joey's eating habits and sensory obsessions

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Chandler, there's a gas leak in my apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's just you.

jokes.text: Hi. He's doing it, he's breaking down the door.
jokes.setup: Joey on phone while destruction happens in background
jokes.punchline: His casual sports commentary tone about property destruction
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Joey's casual tone and presumably dramatic destruction

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I'm still here. No, everything's fine. Why? Oh, that? That's just... that's just the radiator. Yeah, it does that. No, the ceiling's supposed to be like that. Look, I gotta go. Gotta go!

jokes.text: -Okay, Rachel, are you comfortable? -If I said I was, would you judge me?
jokes.setup: Nurse asking if Rachel is comfortable during gynecological exam
jokes.punchline: Rachel's self-aware question about the appropriateness of being comfortable
jokes.explanation: Clever line about the awkwardness of medical exams with self-deprecating awareness

dialogue.speaker: Nurse
dialogue.line: Are you comfortable?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, no. I mean, how comfortable can you be with a man you just met having his hands all over your... you know.

jokes.text: Oh, man, I swear, if they sold these at Pottery Barn..
jokes.setup: Rachel experiencing the hospital gown/equipment
jokes.punchline: Her materialistic observation about medical equipment as home decor
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel materialism making light of medical situation

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this monitor is so cool! Do you think they sell these at Design Within Reach?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And these IV stands? Very minimalist. Very now.

jokes.text: Please, I really.. I want to apologize for the way I acted earlier today. -That's fine. Can you stay up near my head?
jokes.setup: Ross trying to apologize during Rachel's gynecological exam
jokes.punchline: Rachel's practical request about positioning during intimate medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from inappropriate timing of serious conversation

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, I'm so sorry about what I said. I feel terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, you can apologize, but you're going to have to do it from over there. I'm not exactly in a position to move right now.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Right, of course. I just wanted you to know—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Actually, if you're going to keep talking, could you at least stand where I can see you? This angle is not doing you any favors.

jokes.text: -What? Oh, yeah, sorry. -Okay, head, Ross! Head, Ross!
jokes.setup: Ross not staying in appropriate position during exam
jokes.punchline: Rachel's increasingly frantic directions like training a dog
jokes.explanation: Escalating cringe with Rachel treating Ross like a poorly trained pet

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, you need to stay still for the doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, no, no — to the left. Your other left!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, good boy. Good boy. Now sit. Sit!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Who's a good patient? Who's a good patient? You are!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No treats until after the exam, Ross!

jokes.text: Although, we can worry about that after we get married. -Married? -Well, yeah, I think we should get married.
jokes.setup: Ross listing pregnancy preparations
jokes.punchline: His casual assumption they'll get married dropped mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good Ross presumption delivered casually as if obvious conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So we need to start thinking about the nursery, and daycare, and of course the wedding—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I mean... for when we get married.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, we're not getting married.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh. Right. Yeah, I knew that.

jokes.text: Because that's your answer to everything?
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting marriage as solution
jokes.punchline: Rachel calling out Ross's pattern of proposing marriage as fix for problems
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Ross's pattern of using marriage as solution to any relationship problem

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. Every time something goes wrong in your life, you propose to someone?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not true!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You proposed to Emily, you proposed to Rachel, you proposed to Bonnie...

jokes.text: -Excuse me? -You can't even eat alone in a restaurant.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming Rachel can't handle single parenthood
jokes.punchline: His specific, insulting example about her dining habits
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult that escalates the argument with specific behavior

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You can't handle single parenthood. You don't even know how to make a proper meal. You'd probably feed the baby takeout every night.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That is not true! I'm perfectly capable of—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, last week you microwaved a salad.

jokes.text: -I can too eat by myself! -When have you ever? When certain people leave the table and I am not finished!
jokes.setup: Rachel defending her independence
jokes.punchline: Her defensive example actually proves Ross's point
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Rachel's defense inadvertently supports Ross's argument

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't need a man to take care of me. I'm completely independent.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Name one thing you've done completely on your own.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I... I got my own apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Your father pays your rent.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I bought my own furniture!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica helped you pick it out and Chandler carried it up the stairs.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. But I got myself this job!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You asked Monica's boss for it at Thanksgiving.

jokes.text: Well, certain other people take two hours to eat a bowl of soup!
jokes.setup: Defending her eating pace
jokes.punchline: Rachel's counter-attack about Ross's slow eating during emotional argument
jokes.explanation: Good escalation turning the eating criticism back on Ross

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, so now you're attacking the way I eat? That's rich coming from you, Mr. I-Chew-Each-Bite-Forty-Times!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's called proper digestion, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's called being boring! By the time you finish one plate, I've already had dessert and moved on with my life!

jokes.text: Oh, please. You inhale your food. I grew up with Monica. If you didn't eat fast, you didn't eat!
jokes.setup: Rachel criticizing his eating speed
jokes.punchline: Ross's explanation referencing Monica's competitive eating habits
jokes.explanation: Great callback explaining Ross's eating trauma via Monica's competitive family dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you grew up with Monica, you'd understand that if you don't eat fast, you don't eat.

jokes.text: Am I interrupting?
jokes.setup: Doctor walking in on heated argument during medical exam
jokes.punchline: Doctor's polite understatement about obvious inappropriate situation
jokes.explanation: Good professional understatement highlighting the inappropriate timing

dialogue.line: Doctor's polite understatement about obvious inappropriate situation

jokes.text: But not the husband, because, evidently, she can do this alone.
jokes.setup: Introducing himself to the doctor as the father
jokes.punchline: Ross's bitter addition emphasizing their argument to medical professional
jokes.explanation: Good cringe with Ross airing relationship drama to confused medical professional

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm the father.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We're not together anymore.

jokes.text: -Why you can't admit you need me? -I need you to stay near my head!
jokes.setup: Ross asking why she won't admit needing him
jokes.punchline: Rachel's literal interpretation about physical positioning during exam
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning emotional plea into practical medical instruction

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Why won't you just admit that you need me?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't need you, Ross. I can stand on my own two feet.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not what I meant.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, during the exam, I was literally sitting down the whole time.

jokes.text: Pretty amazing, huh? I don't see it!
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on seeing their baby on ultrasound
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate confession that she can't see it
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of emotional moment with Rachel's honest confusion

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Look at that! That's our baby!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't see it.

jokes.text: But you just said that you did. I know. I lied. I didn't want her to think I was a terrible mother.
jokes.setup: Ross pointing out Rachel's contradiction
jokes.punchline: Rachel's admission she lied to doctor about maternal bonding
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's insecurity about motherhood

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You told the doctor you had maternal bonding with the baby?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't want him to think I was a bad mother!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So you lied to your doctor?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I was hormonal, Ross! I panic-bonded!

jokes.text: I can't even see my own baby!
jokes.setup: Rachel's confession about lying
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic escalation about failing as a mother before birth
jokes.explanation: Rachel's tendency to dramatize minor problems into major character flaws

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I lied! I'm not actually pregnant. I just wanted to see if you guys would still love me.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And now I've failed as a mother before I even had a child. I'm the worst person alive.

jokes.text: -Do you really? -No, I don't see it!
jokes.setup: Ross asking if Rachel can really see it now
jokes.punchline: Rachel's continued honest admission of not seeing baby
jokes.explanation: Continuation of running gag about Rachel's inability to see ultrasound

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Can you see it now?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, I still can't see it.

jokes.text: You see this tiny thing that looks like a peanut? Yeah. Sweetie, that's it. That's it? Well, I saw that.
jokes.setup: Ross pointing out the baby on ultrasound
jokes.punchline: Rachel's deflated realization she had seen it but expected something more impressive
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment with Rachel's underwhelmed reaction to tiny fetus

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Look, there's the baby. See the head right there?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I see it! Oh wait... that's it?

jokes.text: I was trying to figure out how bad I want one of Monica's turkey pot pies.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about apartment security after door replacement
jokes.punchline: Her admission she's considering breaking in again for food
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe logic weighing security versus food desires

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So the new lock is really secure? Because I was thinking about breaking in again for food.

jokes.text: -Hey, show them the picture of your uterus. I don't see the baby. Where is it?
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting they show the ultrasound picture
jokes.punchline: Monica having the same problem as Rachel seeing the baby
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the ultrasound confusion to other characters

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We should show everyone the ultrasound picture.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't see it either.

jokes.text: Oh, no, I know. I couldn't see it either at first. But it's right.. Ross, I lost it again.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to help Monica see the baby
jokes.punchline: Rachel admitting she's lost sight of it again despite just learning where it was
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of running gag with Rachel immediately losing track again

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, no, it's right there. See? Right there by the window.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Where? I don't see it.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Right there! By the curtain!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I lost it again.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You just looked at it!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know! I know where it was, and then I looked away for like two seconds...

jokes.text: My way totally works. I feel totally relaxed and calm. Go, go, go!
jokes.setup: Monica claiming her approach to travel works well
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself with frantic urgency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica contradiction between claimed calm and actual frantic energy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My way of packing is very efficient. I have a system.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, what day do we leave? Oh my God, oh my God! We're not ready! Nothing is packed! Why is nothing packed?!

jokes.text: - Mrs. Bing. - It is a funny name.
jokes.setup: Airline agent using Monica's new married name
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating agreement about his surname
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler self-awareness about his awkward surname

dialogue.speaker: Airline Agent
dialogue.line: Ms. Bing?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's actually Mrs. Bing now.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and she's not happy about it.

jokes.text: See, if we've gotten here earlier, those seats might not have been available. Compromise works. My name is "Mrs. Bing". Haven't I compromised enough?
jokes.setup: Chandler pointing out compromise worked this time
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic claim that taking his name was sufficient compromise
jokes.explanation: Great Monica escalation treating name change as ultimate sacrifice

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: See? Compromise works.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh yes, because taking your last name was such a small thing for me to give up.

jokes.text: My anklet. That was a close one.
jokes.setup: Security checkpoint drama
jokes.punchline: Monica's relief that her minor jewelry caused the security alert
jokes.explanation: Monica's dramatic relief over minor security inconvenience

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, thank God it was just my bracelet! I was so worried they were going to find something actually suspicious.

jokes.text: Don't worry about me, I take my bombs very seriously.
jokes.setup: Security warning about bomb jokes
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate violation of the rule he just read
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - reading rule against bomb jokes then immediately making bomb joke

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: We need to warn you, making jokes about bombs is strictly prohibited.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that sucks. Because I've got a million of 'em.

jokes.text: This is ridiculous. I was just making a joke. I know the sign says, "No jokes about bombs", but shouldn't the sign really say, "No Bombs"? Isn't that the guy we really have to worry about here? The guy with the bombs? Not the guy who jokes about his bombs? Not that I have bombs, but if I did, I probably wouldn't joke about them.
jokes.setup: Chandler in trouble for bomb joke
jokes.punchline: His logical explanation that somehow makes him sound more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler escalation - trying to logic his way out while making everything worse

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wasn't making a bomb joke! I was talking about how my dating life is like a time bomb—it's going to explode at some point, and when it does, there will be collateral damage.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, that doesn't help.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? I'm just saying that I have the timing of a detonation, the precision of a professional demolitions expert, and I've been reading a lot about pressure points and weak structural integrity lately.

jokes.text: - He's my husband. - I see. Oh, but only for a few days. I don't know what he does outside of the house.
jokes.setup: Security asking about her relationship to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Monica's attempt to distance herself by emphasizing how new the marriage is
jokes.explanation: Great Monica self-preservation throwing Chandler under the bus while technically honest

dialogue.speaker: Security
dialogue.line: What's your relationship to Chandler Bing?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, well, we're married. But it's very new!

jokes.text: You see, this is the first time we're traveling my way, so it's very important we make this flight. It's her plan versus my plan. What do you mean by "plan"?
jokes.setup: Monica trying to explain their travel methodology
jokes.punchline: Security's suspicious question about the word 'plan' making everything sound worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with innocent travel discussion sounding suspicious to security

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We always plan ahead. We have a plan for everything.

dialogue.speaker: Security Officer
dialogue.line: You have a plan?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, not a good plan.

jokes.text: It took me forever to do this. There's like over 50 folded pieces. You're mean.
jokes.setup: Security unpacking Monica's perfectly organized luggage
jokes.punchline: Monica's childish complaint about her organization being destroyed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica obsessiveness being destroyed by security, childish reaction

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, no, no! You're messing up my system! Everything has a place!

dialogue.speaker: Security
dialogue.line: Ma'am, we have to check your luggage.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But I color-coded everything! See? All the blues together, all the greens together. And my socks are rolled, not folded!

dialogue.speaker: Security
dialogue.line: I understand, but—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You don't understand! This is chaos! Pure chaos!

jokes.text: About those speedos, I don't know how they got in there.
jokes.setup: Being released by security
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward reference to speedos being found in luggage
jokes.explanation: Good callback to opening scene with Chandler's awkward speedo denial

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more embarrassed? They found a speedo in my luggage!

tmdb_overview: Chandler and Monica prepare to leave for their honeymoon as Rachel prepares to tell Ross that he's the father of her baby. While traveling, Monica and Chandler become jealous of another honeymooning couple who get bumped to first class on the airplane. Meanwhile, Joey and Phoebe scheme to get inside the newlyweds' apartment after discovering that they don't have a key.
insight: Eighty-seven jokes in 22 minutes, but emotional weight drags humor impact below season average.
